behavior_summary: Provides simple interaction handlers for a Basket item: when examined it announces itself, and when used it toggles or opens/closes a UI 'gump' depending on an internal status bit.
BASKET::look produces a short, visible text ('basket ') by invoking the engine's Item::bark routine using the item instance as the source/actor.

BASKET::use queries the item's status via Item::getStatus(), tests bit 0x0100 of the returned status word, and either calls Item::openGump(this, 0x0E) when the bit is set or Item::closeGump(this) when the bit is clear.
notable_control_flow: Event-driven: handlers are run in response to discrete events — Event 0 (U8P_BASKET::look) calls Item::bark; Event 1 (use) reads status and branches to open or close a gump.

Lightweight call sequence in U8P_BASKET::look: prepare context, construct literal 'basket ', call Item::bark, free the temporary string, then perform a suspend/implies sequence before returning (inferred engine housekeeping).
Guard-like test in use: a single status word ([BP-02h]) is read and masked against 0x0100 to decide which UI call to invoke (openGump vs closeGump).
open_questions: What semantic meaning does status bit 0x0100 encode for basket items (e.g., 'has gump open', 'wants open', 'special mode')? The script treats it as a toggle condition but the precise game-state meaning is inferred.
What exact side-effects do the implied/suspend instructions after Item::bark produce in the engine (are they purely scheduling/cleanup or do they alter process state)?
Are there any other events or hidden handlers related to this basket item (e.g., drag/drop, pickup) elsewhere in the package that modify the 0x0100 bit?

main_function.name: U8P_BASKET::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player/actor 'U8P_BASKET::look' (examine) action for a Basket item by announcing/displaying the short item text 'basket '.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_BASKET::look) — triggered when the Avatar or relevant actor examines/looks at the basket item.

function_map.name: U8P_BASKET::look
function_map.role: Event handler for 'U8P_BASKET::look' that prepares context and invokes Item::bark to display 'basket '.

function_map.name: U8P_BASKET::use
function_map.role: Event handler for 'use' that reads item status and opens or closes the item's gump (UI) based on bit 0x0100.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine routine to emit/display a short text message coming from an item/process.

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: Engine routine returning item status/flags (word) used to drive UI/logic decisions.

function_map.name: Item::closeGump
function_map.role: Engine routine to close an item's UI/gump.

function_map.name: Item::openGump
function_map.role: Engine routine to open an item's UI/gump; called here with gump id 0x0E (14).

external_calls.purpose: Cause the basket item to emit the short descriptive text 'basket ' to the inspecting process/player.
Retrieve the item's status flags (word) to decide whether to open or close the UI/gump.
Close the basket's UI/gump when the tested status bit (0x0100) is clear.
Open the basket's UI/gump with id 0x0E when the tested status bit (0x0100) is set.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides simple interaction handlers for a Basket item: when examined it announces itself, and when used it toggles or opens/closes a UI 'gump' depending on an internal status bit.
